howItWorks.intro: This rv generator size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Running watts = sum of all appliance wattages
Starting watts = running watts + AC startup surge (~1200W)
Generator size should exceed starting watts by 10-20%
Fuel use ≈ 0.15 gal/hr per 1000W running load
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What size generator do I need for my RV?
faq.answer: Most RVs with one AC unit need a 3,000-3,500W generator. Two AC units require 5,500W+. If you only need lights, fridge, and charging, a 2,000W inverter generator is sufficient.

faq.question: Inverter vs conventional generator for RV?
faq.answer: Inverter generators are quieter (50-60 dB vs 70-80 dB), produce cleaner power for electronics, and are more fuel-efficient. They cost more but are preferred at campgrounds with noise restrictions.

faq.question: How much fuel does an RV generator use?
faq.answer: A 3,500W generator at 50% load uses about 0.25-0.5 gallons per hour. Running a generator 8 hours a day costs roughly $10-15 in fuel at current prices.

faq.question: Is the RV Generator Size Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the RV Generator Size Calculator?
sections.body: The RV Generator Size Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your air conditioner, microwave, fridge on electric, you get instant results including running watts needed, starting watts needed, recommended generator. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting running watts needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your air conditioner and need to find the right running watts needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
